2606.03628.title: Building Reliable Long-Form Generation via Hallucination Rejection Sampling
2606.03628.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) have achieved remarkable progress in open-ended text generation, yet they remain prone to hallucinating incorrect or unsupported content, which undermines their reliability. This issue is exacerbated in long-form generation due to hallucination snowballing, a phenomenon where early errors propagate and compound into subsequent outputs. To address this challenge, we propose a novel inference-time hallucination mitigation framework, named Segment-wise HAllucination Rejection Sampling (SHARS), which uses an arbitrary hallucination detector to identify and reject hallucinated segments during generation and resample until faithful content is produced. By retaining only confident information and building subsequent generations upon it, the framework mitigates hallucination accumulation and enhances factual consistency. To instantiate this framework, we adopt semantic uncertainty as the detector and introduce several vital modifications to address its limitations and better adapt it to long-form text. Our method enables models to self-correct hallucinations without requiring external resources such as web search or knowledge bases, while remaining compatible with them for future extensions. Empirical evaluations on standardized hallucination benchmarks demonstrate that our method substantially reduces hallucinations in long-form generation while preserving or even improving the informativeness of generation. Code is available at: https://github.com/TreeLLi/hallucination-rejection-sampling.
2606.03628.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在開放式文本生成方面取得了顯著的進展，但它們仍然容易產生不正確或不支持的內容，這削弱了它們的可靠性。這個問題在長篇生成中更加嚴重，因為幻覺的滾雪球效應，這是一種早期錯誤在後續輸出中傳播和累積的現象。為了解決這一挑戰，我們提出了一種新穎的推理時幻覺緩解框架，名為段落式幻覺拒絕抽樣（SHARS），該框架使用任意的幻覺檢測器來識別和拒絕生成過程中的幻覺段落，並重新抽樣直到產生忠實的內容。通過僅保留可靠的信息並在其基礎上構建後續生成，該框架減少了幻覺的累積並增強了事實一致性。為了實現這一框架，我們採用了語義不確定性作為檢測器，並引入了幾個重要的修改以解決其局限性，更好地適應長篇文本。我們的方法使模型能夠自我修正幻覺，而無需外部資源，如網絡搜索或知識庫，同時仍然與它們兼容，以便未來擴展。在標準化的幻覺基準上進行的實證評估表明，我們的方法在長篇生成中顯著減少了幻覺，同時保持甚至提高了生成的資訊量。代碼可在以下網址獲取：https://github.com/TreeLLi/hallucination-rejection-sampling。
2606.03628.author: Lin Li et.al.
2606.03628.authors: Lin Li, Georgia Channing, Suhaas M Bhat, Gabriel Davis Jones, Yarin Gal
